name: Preserved Grape Vine Leaves
recipeIngredient: 24 grape vine leaves, medium light color
recipeInstructions:
1. Pick the grape vine leaves early in their growth period during early summer when the vines are covered with leaves.
2. Choose leaves that are not too young and have a medium light color.
3. If the vines have been sprayed, wait for the recommended harvest period specified by the insecticide manufacturer.
4. Snip off the stem of each leaf.
5. Wash the leaves and stack them in piles of 24, with the shiny side facing up.
6. Roll up the leaves and tie them with string.
7. Bring a large pot of water to a boil and add 1/4 cup salt.
8. Blanch the rolled leaves in batches of 4 for 3 minutes, turning them over occasionally to ensure even blanching.
9. Lift out the rolls using tongs and drain on a clean kitchen towel or paper towels.
10. Repeat the blanching process with any remaining rolls.
11. Prepare the brine by boiling 8 cups of water and dissolving 1 cup of rock salt in it.
12. Pack the blanched leaves upright into warm sterilized jars, leaving some headspace at the top.
13. Pour the hot brine over the leaves, ensuring they are completely covered.
14. Remove any air bubbles by gently pressing on the leaves with a clean utensil.
15. Seal the jars tightly when cool to complete the preservation process.
The brine is sufficient for 20 bundles of leaves. Adjust the quantity accordingly based on the number of bundles being preserved.
Note: The original recipe was sourced from "The Complete Middle East Cookbook" by Tess Mallos and adapted for clarity and consistency in format.
